description: A researcher tracking methods, benchmarks, and design decisions for a retrieval system. Tests factual recall of technical parameters, causal recall of method choices, multi-fact synthesis, and abstention on unmeasured results.

memories.text: LongMemEval is a benchmark for long-term conversational memory: it tests whether a system can recall facts stated across many earlier chat sessions.
LoCoMo is a long-conversation memory benchmark built from very long multi-session dialogues with question-answer probes.
Decision: build a custom profession-based benchmark, because existing benchmarks miss real cross-project recall and causal 'why did we decide' recall that professionals actually rely on.
Context-Heavy's hybrid retrieval fuses full-text search and vector search with Reciprocal Rank Fusion using the constant k=60.
After RRF fusion, a cross-encoder reranker (nvidia/rerank-qa-mistral-4b) reorders the top candidates before the answer is synthesized.
Chunking splits documents into 1200-byte chunks with 200 bytes of overlap, preferring paragraph then sentence boundaries. Each node keeps its two best-matching chunks via max-pooling.
Standard retrieval metrics are nDCG, recall@k, and MRR. Answer quality — faithfulness and abstention correctness — requires an LLM-as-judge with a fixed model and rubric.

questions.question: Why build a custom benchmark instead of only using LongMemEval and LoCoMo?
questions.answer: Because existing benchmarks miss real cross-project recall and causal 'why did we decide' recall that professionals rely on.

questions.question: What fuses full-text and vector search results in Context-Heavy, and with what parameter?
questions.answer: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with the constant k=60.

questions.question: Which reranker model reorders candidates after fusion?
What is Context-Heavy's exact F1 score on the SQuAD benchmark?
Describe Context-Heavy's chunking parameters.
questions.answer: 1200-byte chunks with 200 bytes of overlap on paragraph then sentence boundaries, keeping the two best chunks per node by max-pooling.

questions.question: Which metrics need an LLM-as-judge rather than ranked-list math?
questions.answer: Answer faithfulness and abstention correctness need an LLM judge; recall@k, MRR, and nDCG are computed from the ranked list.
